compiled_state.title: As a DSO agent running brainstorm or preplanning, I can query core design heuristics and federal UX principles to ground design decisions in expert guidance
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Populates plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/principles/ with core design heuristics and federal UX principles: Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics, Don Norman's principles, GOV.UK Government Design Principles, 18F UX Guide principles, digital.gov federal requirements, and Inclusive Design Principles. Manually updates _index.yaml.

**Why**: Brainstorm and preplanning agents need high-level design principles to evaluate feature approaches against expert guidance. This Foundation story delivers the principled guidance layer; Story-5b (Enhancement) delivers the pattern/taxonomy layer.

**Scope**:
- IN: principles/ directory — Nielsen 10 heuristics, Norman key principles, GOV.UK 10 Government Design Principles, 18F UX Guide principles, digital.gov federal requirements, Inclusive Design Principles; Summary + Detail required; license per source
- OUT: Dark patterns, cognitive load, and taxonomies (belong in Story-5b); USWDS/WCAG (Stories 3-4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, principles/ directory covers all 10 Nielsen heuristics, Norman's key principles, all 10 GOV.UK Government Design Principles, 18F UX Guide core principles, digital.gov federal requirements, and Inclusive Design Principles; each entry has YAML frontmatter with domain: [principles] and Summary + Detail tiers; no entry file exceeds 500 lines
  ← Satisfies: SC4 (Nielsen heuristics, Norman principles, GOV.UK principles, 18F UX Guide, digital.gov, Inclusive Design)

- When this story is complete, _index.yaml is manually updated with all principles/ entries and committed; check-corpus-schema.sh passes on all principles/ files
  ← Satisfies: SC1, SC2

- When this story is complete, dso ref-query "design heuristics" returns at least one entry from the principles/ directory
  ← Satisfies: SC4 + SC3

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Multiple source licenses — verify each source: GOV.UK uses OGL v3, 18F/digital.gov are US Government works (public domain), Nielsen material may require paraphrase rather than verbatim copy; record in docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml
- [Reliability] Inclusive Design Principles are published by the Paciello Group (CC-BY); verify license before committing verbatim content

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
